summary.rationale: Mixed employee sentiment; moderate innovation; 40 reviews

reviews.title: Great
reviews.pros:
Understanding 
Easy
Time 
Good 
Right
reviews.cons:
Pto never accommodated 
Point system 
Money 
Scammers
Customers
reviews.advice: No

reviews.title: From an Asst Store Manager
reviews.pros: Advancement opportunities, great at developing skills, great place to learn a skill and grow your resume, or grow with the company.
reviews.cons: Understaffing issues negatively affects all parts of their business. However, Walmart has increased their minimum hourly rate with hopes to attract more committed employees, and retain better employees.
reviews.advice: At the store level, any time you are called in to over ride an associate following company policy, make sure that you first make it clear to the associate and customer that they were doing their job and that you are making an exception.

reviews.title: So happy to be outta there
reviews.pros: There are some cool people
reviews.cons:
Following comments reflect creative marketing team, I don't have much knowledge of other departments.
Your soul will be sucked from your body and they won't pay you very well (salary negotiations are aggressive and they're not flexible). Also hope you either A) don't need healthcare or b) are married to someone with good health insurance. Just look into the 'premium' ins. policy for FTEs, it's shockingly cheap and one of the benefits is there's an HSA attached, which are a regressive alternative to actually providing decent, dignified health coverage.
All they care about is sales performance (i mean, obviously) but it's gotten more gross over the nearly 10 years I worked there. 
If you are a creative marketing professional, go ahead and accept a contractor role but know they will never hire you full time (but you can make more $ as a contractor so get that bag). Trust no one, particularly your manager, because they are all narcs. Just smile and nod.
Creative leadership is ineffective and toxic, which reflects what I believe to be a general rot at that company.
reviews.advice: keep doing what you're doing, nobody likes you anyway.

reviews.title: Net Negative to my Worth
reviews.pros: Plenty of people both customers and associates to engage with.
reviews.cons:
Company policy is designed to replace you. Retaliation for reporting abuse felt guaranteed.
Most Wal-Mart employees live on government assistance.
Never felt appreciated, the company restructured the job titles so they seem underwhelming like this role for instance is for garden center and toys, but it sounds like a holiday contract. This is so you can't get a better job anywhere.
The Wal-Mart sponsored psychiatrist misdiagnosed my ADHD as Bipolar disorder and put me on medication that made me suicidal temporarily instead of letting me focus.
The college dollar a day program scams you out of financial aid, SNHU is cheaper if you get good grades with FAFSA at any other company that is willing to give hours.
The attendance system is cold and designed for management to be able to fire you systemically without notice. I saw this system abused to fire someone who had to take their father to the emergency room. They both worked at the store.
They do nothing to protect their associates against prejudice.
Managers were having affairs with associates. They will write you up over a minute of overtime. They will write you up for cutting overtime. They will write you up for taking your law protected 15 minute breaks. Management will insult you to your face with vulgar language without repercussions, and they do nothing about drug abuse in management.
reviews.advice: Read books written by successful philanthropists that are charismatic. Not the overly satire 48 laws of power. You can often get someone to work hard with a little encouragement as long as you don't follow them around and berate them everyday. Home Office is going to replace you when they can with AI first so get your resume ready.

reviews.title: Your soul dies here
reviews.pros:
-They do have great PTO. Protected PTO can be used however. If you have 8 hrs of protected PTO you can take a whole night off (paid). Or if you're late, it'll cover you too. 
-Clocking in and out and almost anything else can be done from their Walmart employee app.
reviews.cons: Everything else sucks. Managers, people, and realizing how little by little companies try to squeeze every last dime out of consumers.
reviews.advice: If you have good workers, who do their job, show up, and aren't a problem.....you should probably not let them go.

reviews.title: Not That Bad - Good For Money
reviews.pros: This can be a time consuming job or a boring job depending on the time of year, but I enjoy the time I get to help others and not have to be present in my mind while I do the work. It is pretty easy. Sometimes you have to talk to customers and tell them where something is.
reviews.cons: If you're not like me, the job being boring may be a con. It is both for me. I have gotten done before noon or an hour afterwards, and I have to stock aisles, which is the most boring thing here. Everyone is always stressed out, and we have to deal with it.

reviews.title: No life
reviews.pros: Walmart offers paid Tuition for selective fields
reviews.cons:
No outside life- overnights drain you.
Day shift always seems to wake up thinking how they can ream your shift for absolutely no reason. 
No respect from Day shift at all.
reviews.advice: Somehow streamline the negativity viewed from day shift management. Perhaps make day shift salaried alternate spending 1 day a week overnight, this should keep them in reality as they only seem to praise us when they also experience how the night goes.

reviews.title: Lost cause
reviews.pros: Benefits are great for  a company like this.
reviews.cons:
1. Lazy absentee management you will never receive the help you need even if there is an angry customer screaming at you.
2. Pay sucks for the job market, starts at 15 witch in arnold is lower than McDonald's for 4 times the work.
3. Management will make you out to be stupid or mean to the customers, you tell the customer you can't do something due to policy, management will say what policy and will often give customers free product just so they will leave without more complaining,
4. Little to no raises even with good reviews the max is $0.50.
5. Does not follow any of its own policy, you will not get full time even if you were hired at full time, they do not follow team scedualing or hard availability.
reviews.advice:
Follow company policy.
Stop giving customers free stuff so you don't have to deal with them, it makes the employee who was dealing with them look stupid and wrong so the customer think the employee was singling them out for no reason leading to bad reviews for doing job correctly

reviews.title: Ehhhh
reviews.pros: The peers you meet will have your back if you do your job, and the benefits are pretty amazing
reviews.cons: The lack of communication is so obvious, there was a policy for the spark drivers that they would have their own lane because per request from management "we are to count every spark diver", now they just let anybody through and it throws you off. Also management is ridiculously snooty.
reviews.advice: Please having group meeting with the cashiers, they deserve to be on the same page. Please be more active on front end, if someone walks off from self checkout (because they do) then you need to be there. Hold everyone accountable,  and dont retaliate when its our time to go home or clock out.

reviews.title: Review of home office
reviews.pros: Was able to work remote. Managers allowed some flexibility and wanted to assist us with understanding certain concepts of payroll.
reviews.cons: Associates working on taking calls couldn't resolve certain issues which could've been resolved by asking certain questions. They sent our jobs overseas so we couldn't keep our jobs. They wanted certain requirements that some associates couldn't keep up with such as calls per minute and audits.
reviews.advice: Work with associates on how to resolve issues the employee is asking.

reviews.title: Meh
reviews.pros: They keep you in hours for the most part. The coaches are ok.
reviews.cons: No heart, the a.i. for scheduling is horrible, the absence policies are bad. The customers are horrible. They pay a couple bucks more because you deal with customers. Its not enough. Night shift takes all the BS and works harder than anyone in the store. It was different when I started 4 people all the time, now its 3 people and I do the work of the missing person, or we go down to 2 people and i do the work of 3.
reviews.advice: A.i. scheduling is dumb. Night shift deli needs 4 people on because people call out all the time.

reviews.title: Convenient but overwhelming
reviews.pros: If you work well, you get more hours. Discount card after a couple of months. Better pay than most retail stores.
reviews.cons: If you work well, they overload you with more work you’re unable to finish because you’ll be pulled somewhere else to finish someone else’s unfinished work. There’s sometimes an implication of repercussions if you don’t finish all your work. Management protects themselves even when there was inappropriate and imbalanced behavior between them and the associates. They will side with a customer even if that customer threatens you.
reviews.advice: Treat your staff like people and know that while someone works well, they are only there to work and are not being paid the same amount for management work they are sometimes assigned. They do not have the same dedication or responsibilities as one would in management.

reviews.title: Work at Walmart if you have zero alternatives or you just enjoy being stressed
reviews.pros:
Got paid $17 hr which helped as a single mom living rurally.
Work friends.
reviews.cons: Hours would be randomly cut every Spring.  We are timed and tracked, coach always made it feel like we needed to go even faster.  So stressful to me, as it's hard to push a heavy cart full of groceries around quickly when there are customers EVERYWHERE.  I can't even speak about doing the job during the holidays without having PTSD flashbacks lol.
reviews.advice: Remember that we are human beings.

reviews.title: Spark Driver
reviews.pros: Make your own schedule, make up to $200 plus per day. Get paid multiple times daily, as soon as each order is delivered. Free Walmart plus if you hit your monthly metrics (very easy to hit).
reviews.cons: Need to have carts or bags to prepare for big orders going up flights of stairs because sometimes customers will not help. Some of the stores move extremely slow and constantly cancel orders. Cancelled orders pay like $2, even if you sat there for 30+ minutes waiting and the store associates decide to cancel the order. Sometime the app glitches.

reviews.title: Walmart Associate
reviews.pros: Flexible Scheduling, some nice team leads and coaches
reviews.cons: Pay raise scaling is ridiculous and only bumps your pay barely over what inflation has already taken out of your earnings. And that's only if you achieve the requirement. Other than that, your pay scaling falls below inflation.
reviews.advice: Hire competent team leads and associates that are not lazy and leaves others to do work. One team lead in particular will stand around on their phone while claiming to "not be babying" the associates. Then this individual just doesn't do any work, provide direction, or step in to help when rushes occur and instead tells other people already doing something to stop and help out.

reviews.title: OPD worker
reviews.pros: the community is great at Walmart
reviews.cons: The head management are cold and will turn a stiff shoulder to you whenever you mess up, even if its on their end. So make sure to keep up with little mistakes to keep yourself away from trouble.

reviews.title: Used to be a good workplace, not sure about it anymore
reviews.pros: The OPD management when I worked there were actually really cool. I had a great relationship with all the managers and team leads and found this job to be rather enjoyable while there. I walked in with a dress shirt, pants, and nice shoes and talked to the hiring manager and got the job with no experience as well.
reviews.cons: I recently went back there as a customer and it turns out pretty much the entire department quit over the course of a year after I left. I would not recommend working OPD as I found my old manager in a different department and he said it is terrible now.

reviews.title: Solid
reviews.pros: I get paid pretty well for the work that I do
reviews.cons: They do not work with your schedule.
reviews.advice: If I say I can’t work a day on my availability, then I can’t work it.

reviews.title: Good But Depends on Managers
reviews.pros: If managers are good and if they don't take out their life on you, it's not a bad job at all. Most of my coworkers are awesome and actually make the job better.
reviews.cons: Some managers take a power trip. Also not that flexible of a schedule and you will get your schedule 3 weeks in advance. So if you are a part time employee, you have to plan at LEAST a month in advance to do anything.
reviews.advice: Just be there, do your job, and leave. We do care about you to an extent, but if you start taking it out on employees or just don't do your job, people will stop caring about you.

reviews.title: Stocker
reviews.pros: Ok depending on store and leadership. Either one can make or break the job.
reviews.cons: Toxic leadership that will never get fired.
reviews.advice: Give more money and food in break room.

reviews.title: Good Company
reviews.pros:
PTO
Medical  benefits
College tuition coverage
Promotes from within when possible
reviews.cons:
No work life balance at this level
Lacks mentoring from supervisors at times
reviews.advice: Need to be a self-motivator and have responsibility for some self developed.  Create a routine for time management.

reviews.title: Great work not bad at all
reviews.pros:
It was good structural for points.
 And overall Walmart system is easy understand and hold everyone accountable where needed
reviews.cons: I don't know Walmart is impossible to hold of to get a job that's hiring in my area has been for months but just can't get a hold of anyone it's unsettling
reviews.advice: Nanana

reviews.title: Innovative, Growth-Oriented Organization with a Beautiful New Campus
reviews.pros: Walmart is a forwarding thinking and actively embracing technology and AI to drive business and ultimately support it's customers. There are many opportunities for advancement and professional growth. Walmart recently opened a brand-new, state of the art campus that offers modern, collaborative, and impressive work environment. Overall the leadership promotions innovation and customer centric future focused mindset.
reviews.cons: Some advancement opportunities maybe limited to Bentonville, AR which maybe limiting for employees seeking more geographical flexibility. Most roles require being in the office 5 days a week. While the campus is located in charming small town, it may not appear to those who prefer a larger metropolitan lifestyle.

reviews.title: Quality of Work Life + Work Life Balance Depends on Team
reviews.pros:
- pay is decent for Arkansas, not compared to coastal states 
- new campus is nice
- some teams will promote quicker
reviews.cons:
- experience seems to be highly dependent on your org and your direct line chain of command
- a lot of teams are propped up by a few contributors while everyone else coasts

reviews.title: Walmart Associate
reviews.pros: I work 3rd shift overnight and there is rarely customer interaction and that's a pro. Plus there is 1.50 differential per hour in pay.
reviews.cons: We have hardly any staff on 3rd shift so there is alot more work for those of us who are there on 3rd shift.
reviews.advice: Hire more employees for 3rd shift.

reviews.title: Decent Job
reviews.pros: Consistent Work, Managers are friendly and helpful
reviews.cons: Not a lot of opportunties for growth

reviews.title: GREAT JOB
reviews.pros: Good pay , Flexible Hours, Great Management, Opportunity for overtime + more hours.
reviews.cons: On your feet alot but rewarding job overall
reviews.advice: N/A

reviews.title: Great for only a TEMP Job… BUT
reviews.pros:
- early pay days (one@work app)
- payday loans with no outrageous fees 
- consistent schedule
reviews.cons:
- EXTREMELY LOW PAY - to do strenuous work.
- Limited communication amongst management. 
- Feels like Highschool 2.0 
- NO DISCOUNT for duration of temporary job, not fair.
- POINTS SYSTEM - unfair 1000%
- The fact that you guys don’t accept doctors notes (as a whole organization),…
reviews.advice:
If you guys are going to stay with the point system, at least be a bit more lenient. Some of us are dealing with our own mental health and family health issues/struggles. 
- ACCEPT DOCTORS NOTES. It might help keep employees who really need their jobs.
- Make Temporary employees feel special and offer them a discount too.

reviews.title: It was okay, but could have been better.
reviews.pros:
- set schedule
- decent pay
- discount was nice
reviews.cons:
- overworked, and not enough help
- discounts got extremely selective, and less and less products were given discounts on
-management was terrible
-rude, entitled customers
reviews.advice: Stop sitting on your butts in the office, and get out on the floor and help associates out instead of harping on them for not getting things done. When you're practically the only one on the floor, and you have to answer to customers constantly in 3-4 different departments, and then also do everything else, it becomes extremely overwhelming and stressful.

reviews.title: Good starting job
reviews.pros: Easy work and good for a part-time job or if you don't have another job lined up.
reviews.cons: Can be boring, and depending on your management can have a better or worse experience.

reviews.title: Good job
reviews.pros: Very good to there employees
reviews.cons: Salary and hours could be better

reviews.title: Not great
reviews.pros: Get hired without an interview, system takes availability seriously, employee discount on groceries, patch your tires for free if you bought tires from them
reviews.cons: If they told you that Walmart+ is free they are lying because they consistently took out $15 a month out of my paycheck, management is hit or miss, so many coworkers mess around and don't do their jobs, being on your feet consistently all day, various team leads always telling you something different
reviews.advice: The whole store should be working as a team, not various individuals all deciding things differently.

reviews.title: It's a job.
reviews.pros: You make more than grocery stockers at other businesses.
reviews.cons: Workload is heavy and the management is toxic.

reviews.title: Okay place to work but can’t grow past a certain point
reviews.pros: Good job fields (if you’re in the pharmacy or optical or OPD), long lunches, sometimes good leadership.
reviews.cons: Lack of availability to move up. In my position specifically after lead tech that’s it. Unless you want to become a pharmacist or corporate you can’t get any higher. Also most people who have been with the company for a long period of time when they started the new pay structure basically capped out within the first year of the new pay structure. With no way to advance and no more raises what is the reason for staying? The new “bonus” payout is absolutely horrible as well. The absence system is also HORRIBLE. Also if you’re an immunizing technician you don’t even get anything extra for providing that, if you work in a test to treat store you don’t get any extra pay for doing flu, covid or strep tests so good luck if someone throws up on you during a strep test.
reviews.advice: Listen to your employees, understand that certain positions you can’t climb a ladder past a certain point and not everyone wants to be corporate so pay accordingly and not blanket pay. It gives no one any incentive to want to stay.

reviews.title: It is Alright
reviews.pros:
Consistent scheduling
Great growth opportunities
Good benefits
reviews.cons:
Cons can vary from store to store.
Pushed to work to hard and strain yourself.
Neglect from management on issues

reviews.title: Walmart team lead
reviews.pros: Good team, good learning and good pay
reviews.cons: Work schedule is poor and lots of overtime

reviews.title: Overall good
reviews.pros: People are nice.    Pretty good benefits after six months.  Working better on employee morale.   Trying to communicate more with employees.
reviews.cons: Some times don’t feel appreciated.  Multiple different opinions on how to do things. Not on same page usually.  Sometimes don’t follow through with rules or changes we want to make.
reviews.advice: Work together start communication with each team lead and on same page.

reviews.title: team member
reviews.pros: good place to gain experience
reviews.cons: Plenty of better options elsewhere for development and opportunities

reviews.title: Middle of the Road
reviews.pros: Flexibility to determine my work schedule
reviews.cons: The pay was not very good

reviews.title: Innovative, People-Focused, Lots of opportunities
reviews.pros:
Lots of internal opportunities
Great culture, promotes people-first and servant leadership culture 
Competitive pay and strong total comp with bonus and stock; good benefits
reviews.cons:
Constant restructuring
In some departments, too many decision makers and role overlaps
